body:
ü§ñ *This is an automated draft PR from [Perf Improver](https://github.com/iamnbutler/crdt/actions/runs/23490833303), an AI-powered performance optimization assistant.*

## Goal and Rationale

Two hot-path optimizations in `TextBuffer` targeting collaborative editing performance:

1. **`hasFragment` O(1) index** ‚Äì Called for every incoming remote operation to check causal readiness. The previous O(n) implementation iterated all fragments and allocated a new array on each call via `fragmentsArray()`. Under network delay or reordering, every pending operation pays this cost repeatedly.

2. **`appliedOps` numeric map** ‚Äì Previously used `Set<string>` with template-literal keys like `` `${replicaId}:${counter}` ``. Every idempotency check (and `retryPendingOps` loop) created a new string. Replaced with `Map<number, Set<number>>` for allocation-free numeric lookups.

## Approach

### `hasFragment` index
- Added `_fragmentIds: Map<number, Set<number>>` (replicaId ‚Üí Set\<counter\>) field
- Added `setFragments(frags: Fragment[]): void` helper that atomically rebuilds both the `SumTree` and the index ‚Äî all former `this.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(...)` callsites now go through this helper
- `hasFragment()` reduced to: `return this._fragmentIds.get(rid)?.has(counter) ?? false`

### `appliedOps` numeric map
- Changed field from `Set<string>` to `Map<number, Set<number>>`
- Added `hasAppliedOp(id)` and `markAppliedOp(id)` helpers
- **Serialization format unchanged** ‚Äî at `toSerializedSnapshot()` / `fromSerializedSnapshot()` boundaries we still convert to/from `string[]` for backward compatibility

## Performance Evidence

Benchmark: apply 500 operations in **reverse order** to a fresh buffer (worst case ‚Äî all ops pend until dependencies arrive, maximising `hasFragment` + `appliedOps` call frequency).

| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| ¬µs/op | 596.20 | 62.68 | **9.5√ó faster** |
| ops/sec | 1,677 | 15,953 | **9.5√ó throughput** |

Methodology:
```ts
// Build 500 insert ops on buf1
// Apply them in reverse order to a fresh buf
// 1000 iterations, wall-clock time
```

## Trade-offs

- **Memory**: `_fragmentIds` holds one `Set<number>` per distinct `replicaId` in the document. For typical collaborative sessions (2‚Äì10 replicas), this is negligible.
- **`setFragments` rebuild cost**: The index rebuild is O(n fragments) ‚Äî same asymptotic cost as `SumTree.fromItems`, so no additional order of complexity. Constant factor is small (one Map lookup + Set insert per fragment).
- **Serialization stays backward compatible**: `appliedOps` wire format is still `string[]`.

## Reproducibility

```bash
# Install bun, then:
bun install
bun test   # 409 pass
```

Micro-benchmark script available at `benchmarks/` directory (see `bench:text` script).

## Test Status

‚úÖ 409 tests pass, 0 failures  
‚úÖ No new lint errors introduced (3 pre-existing `noNonNullAssertion` warnings in same file unchanged)  
‚úÖ TypeScript clean on modified file (`tsc --noEmit` shows no errors in `text-buffer.ts`)
title: perf: O(1) hasFragment lookup + numeric appliedOps map

body:
ü§ñ *Perf Improver here - I'm an automated AI assistant focused on performance improvements for this repository.*

## Activity for March 2026

## Suggested Actions for Maintainer

* [ ] **Review PR** (pending creation): `perf: O(1) hasFragment lookup + numeric appliedOps map` - 9.5√ó speedup for `applyRemote` with out-of-order ops - [Review once created](https://github.com/iamnbutler/crdt/pulls)

## Performance Opportunities Backlog

1. **`hasFragment` O(1) index** ‚úÖ Implemented this run (9.5√ó speedup)
2. **`appliedOps` numeric map** ‚úÖ Implemented this run
3. **`textbuffer-array`** - `fragmentsArray()` allocates new array on every call; could cache or use iterator API
4. **`shallow-clone` summaries copy** - `shallowClone()` does `new Map(this.summaries)` which is O(n nodes); could use copy-on-write
5. **Pending ops `isCausallyReady` loop** - scans all pending ops on every applied op; could use dependency graph

## Discovered Commands

```bash
bun install --frozen-lockfile   # Install dependencies
bun test                         # Run all tests (409 pass)
bun test --test-name-pattern "^(?!.*CRDT Property)"  # Skip slow property tests
bun run typecheck               # TypeScript check
bun run lint                    # Biome linting
bun run bench                   # Run all benchmarks (requires fixtures)
bun run bench:text:ci           # Text buffer benchmarks (CI mode)
bun run fixtures:download       # Download Kleppmann editing trace (needed for bench)
```

## Run History

### 2026-03-24 - [Run](https://github.com/iamnbutler/crdt/actions/runs/23490833303)
- üîß Implemented `hasFragment` O(1) index via `_fragmentIds: Map<number, Set<number>>`
- üîß Replaced `appliedOps: Set<string>` with `Map<number, Set<number>>` (no string allocation)
- üìä Measured: `applyRemote` with 500 out-of-order ops: **596¬µs ‚Üí 63¬µs (9.5√ó faster)**
- üîß Created draft PR: `[Perf Improver] perf: O(1) hasFragment lookup + numeric appliedOps map`
title: Monthly Activity March 2026
